skill.name: wan-2-7
skill.description: Generate text-to-video with Wan 2.7 (Wan-AI's flagship motion model) on RunComfy. Documents Wan 2.7's strengths (multi-reference conditioning, audio-driven lip-sync via `audio_url`, smoother transitions, prompt expansion), the duration / resolution / aspect-ratio schema, and when to route to HappyHorse 1.0 / Seedance 2.0 / Kling / LTX 2 instead. Calls `runcomfy run wan-ai/wan-2-7/text-to-video` through the local RunComfy CLI. Triggers on "wan", "wan 2.7", "wan-2-7", "wan video", or any explicit ask to generate video with this model.
skill.summary: Text-to-video generation skill that documents Wan 2.7's audio-driven lip-sync, multi-reference conditioning, and the RunComfy CLI invocation pattern.
skill.author: doany-ai

security_audit.summary: Skill is pure Markdown documentation with no executable code. The 72 static findings are dominated by false positives: the 58 'Ruby/shell backtick' flags are all Markdown inline-code backticks (e.g. `audio_url`, `runcomfy run ...`), not Ruby execution. Hardcoded URLs are documentation links to runcomfy.com and the model API. The 'hidden file in home directory' flag at line 181 is the documented, expected token-storage path (mode 0600) called out in the skill's own Security & Privacy section. 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' flags at lines 4/112/162 are spurious keyword matches. Intent and context are benign: the skill wraps a single third-party CLI (`runcomfy`) for text-to-video and explicitly documents the security boundary (HTTPS-only endpoints, env-var token override, 2 GiB download cap, no telemetry).

low_findings.title: Markdown inline backticks misread as shell execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 58 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' hits across SKILL.md. Every match is Markdown inline-code formatting (e.g. `audio_url`, `runcomfy run wan-ai/wan-2-7/text-to-video`, `enable_prompt_expansion: false`). The file contains no Ruby and no shell interpreter; backticks are only used to render literal token names. No execution surface exists.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded documentation URLs
low_findings.description: Seven hardcoded URLs are present, all of which are documentation links: runcomfy.com landing page, the model endpoint page, GitHub repo, a placeholder `https://.../voiceover.mp3` for `audio_url`, the troubleshooting docs page, and the documented `model-api.runcomfy.net` API host. None are used for exfiltration; the only outbound destinations are the documented RunComfy model API and download CDN.

low_findings.title: Documented token storage in home directory
low_findings.description: Line 181 documents that `runcomfy login` stores the API token at `~/.config/runcomfy/token.json` with mode 0600 and offers a `RUNCOMFY_TOKEN` env-var override for CI. This is the standard XDG-style config path, called out explicitly in the skill's own Security & Privacy section. The skill does not read or write the file itself; the RunComfy CLI does, and the env-var path bypasses the file entirely.

low_findings.title: Spurious 'weak cryptographic algorithm' flags
low_findings.description: Lines 4, 112, and 162 were flagged for 'weak cryptographic algorithm' but contain only descriptive prose (model overview, anti-patterns heading, exit-codes table heading). The SKILL.md does not implement or recommend any cryptographic primitive. The matches appear to be keyword-driven false positives.

dangerous_patterns.title: CLI invocation examples with absolute output directory
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill's bash examples use `--output-dir <absolute/path>` as a placeholder for a user-supplied absolute path. The pattern is documentation; the actual substitution is performed by the user, not the skill. No shell expansion of the user prompt occurs (line 182 explicitly notes the CLI transmits the JSON body directly to the API, bypassing shell parsing), so prompt-content injection is not reachable.

content.user_title: Generate Text-to-Video with Wan 2.7 on RunComfy
content.value_statement: Generating broadcast-quality video from text requires precise control over motion, audio, and aspect ratio. Wan 2.7 on RunComfy provides multi-reference conditioning, audio-driven lip-sync, and clean exit codes for production workflows.
actual_capabilities: Generate 2-15 second text-to-video clips with Wan 2.7 at 720p or 1080p resolution
Drive lip-sync by supplying an audio_url (3-30s WAV or MP3, 15MB or less)
Control aspect ratio (16:9, 9:16, 1:1, 4:3, 3:4) and reuse seeds for variant exploration
Use negative_prompt to exclude concrete artifacts such as subtitles, flicker, or distorted hands
Toggle prompt expansion on or off for literal or auto-rewritten short prompts
Stitch multiple calls together to work around the 15-second per-call duration cap
limitations: Single clip is capped at 15 seconds; longer narratives require multiple calls and external stitching
Maximum resolution is 1080p; native 4K output is not supported
Audio inputs must be 3-30 seconds, 15MB or less, and WAV or MP3 format
Reference media is capped at 5 total (image, video, and voice combined)

use_cases.title: Create branded short video ads with custom voiceovers
use_cases.description: Marketing teams can supply their own voice track via audio_url and produce lip-synced spokesperson clips in 9:16 for paid social, then iterate on the seed for variants.

use_cases.title: Produce vertical platform-native spokesperson content
use_cases.description: Social media creators can generate 9:16 lip-synced clips with locked-tripod framing for TikTok, Reels, and Shorts using the documented prompt patterns.

use_cases.title: Integrate text-to-video into CI pipelines
use_cases.description: Developers can call the RunComfy CLI with the RUNCOMFY_TOKEN env var in containers or CI, avoid interactive login, and react to documented exit codes for retries.

prompt_templates.title: Default 5-second product showcase
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 5-second 1080p 16:9 clip of a product on a marble surface with soft studio lighting, a slow subtle camera push-in, and a shallow depth of field.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner — default settings, prompt expansion on

prompt_templates.title: Lip-synced 9:16 spokesperson
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 12-second 9:16 clip of a medium close-up of a confident spokesperson in a softly-lit recording booth, locked tripod, shallow depth of field, warm key light, with my voiceover audio_url.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate — audio_url lip-sync with vertical aspect ratio

prompt_templates.title: Literal-control cinematic shot
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a cinematic 8-second 16:9 clip with enable_prompt_expansion set to false and a negative_prompt of 'no subtitles, no flicker, no distorted hands' to keep the model faithful to the exact wording.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced — disable prompt expansion and target concrete issues

prompt_templates.title: Multi-language dub variants
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate the same 10-second 16:9 prompt three times with different audio_url tracks for English, Spanish, and Japanese, reusing the same seed for visual consistency.
prompt_templates.scenario: Expert — same prompt, varied audio_url, shared seed for variant dubs

output_examples.input: Prompt: 'Cinematic medium shot of a product on a marble surface, soft studio lighting, slow subtle camera push-in, shallow depth of field, premium commercial look, crisp 1080p detail'
output_examples.output: A 5-second 1080p 16:9 clip with subtle camera push-in and shallow depth of field, saved to the configured --output-dir as an MP4.

output_examples.input: Prompt: 'Medium close-up of a confident spokesperson in a softly-lit recording booth, locked tripod, shallow depth of field, warm key light' with audio_url: 'https://.../voiceover.mp3', duration 12, aspect_ratio 9:16
output_examples.output: A 12-second 9:16 clip with lip-sync aligned to the supplied audio track, downloaded to the configured --output-dir.

best_practices: Front-load camera and motion direction in the prompt (for example, 'slow dolly in', 'locked tripod, low angle') for stronger results
Use specific negative_prompt entries such as 'no subtitles, no watermark, no flicker' instead of vague language like 'no bad lighting'
Match audio_url length to the desired clip duration and stay within the 3-30s, 15MB, WAV or MP3 envelope to avoid rejection
anti_patterns: Pile up multiple competing actions in a single prompt; Wan 2.7 performs better with one primary action per clip
Pass audio outside the 3-30s, 15MB, WAV or MP3 spec; the API will reject the request
Use static-frame descriptions without camera or motion cues; the model will produce vague or drifting motion

faq.question: What is Wan 2.7 best at?
faq.answer: Lip-synced video from a supplied audio track, multi-reference motion control, and clean motion physics.

faq.question: How long can a single clip be?
faq.answer: 2 to 15 seconds. Stitch multiple calls for longer narratives.

faq.question: Does Wan 2.7 generate voice audio?
faq.answer: No. It accepts an audio_url for lip-sync. For in-pass voice generation, use Seedance 2.0 Pro.

faq.question: How is the API token stored?
faq.answer: runcomfy login writes the token to ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json with mode 0600. Set RUNCOMFY_TOKEN in CI to bypass the file.

faq.question: Can I reuse the same seed for variants?
faq.answer: Yes. Reuse the seed for consistent output across variants of the same prompt; change it for genuine variety.
